summary.overall: Collaboration-bus is structurally present and live ledgers are valid JSONL, but QA found append-only telemetry truncation risk, schema violations, monitor/counting trust gaps, undefined consensus promotion, and unenforced broadcast adoption.

findings.finding: Collab ledgers are touched with append mode, but child-agents.jsonl initialization uses write/truncate semantics in launcher seeding; commander-002/003 telemetry lacks commander_registered at line 1, proving launch-proof history can be lost.
evidence: bin/stampede.sh child-agents init opens with w mode (worker-reported refs around 853-860)
findings.recommendation: Make child-agents initialization append/idempotent and use an os.open(O_APPEND|O_CREAT|O_WRONLY)+os.write helper for JSONL telemetry.

findings.finding: All 31 live ledger lines parse as JSON, have correct event names, no blank lines, no duplicate item_ids, and trailing newlines; however all peer reviews and some consensus/broadcast items omit required evidence, and source_refs often contain file paths rather than upstream item_ids.
evidence: collab/protocol.json required_fields includes evidence and source_refs
findings.recommendation: Enforce required_fields at write time; separate upstream item_id refs from evidence file paths or clarify protocol semantics.

findings.finding: Monitor uses raw line counts and commander swarm-state, not JSON/schema-valid counts nor child telemetry reconciliation, so live status can overstate health and miss malformed/schema-invalid lines or telemetry divergence.
evidence: bin/stampede-monitor.sh wc -l based ledger counting (worker-reported refs around 32-66)
collab ledgers total 31 lines/31 valid JSON but only 18 schema-valid by required_fields
commander-002 contributed only one collab item in current ledgers
findings.recommendation: Report lines, valid JSON, schema-valid counts, and warn when child-agents launch events diverge from swarm-state.

findings.finding: Consensus entries exist and mostly carry source_refs/confidence, but no automated or documented promotion rule validates when proposals/reviews/improvements become consensus; bundle consumption uses inconsistent adopted consensus field names.
evidence: collab/protocol.json defines workflow sequence but no promotion criteria
commander bundle schema drift reported: adopted_consensus_item_ids vs adopted_consensus
findings.recommendation: Define promotion criteria, require based_on/source item_ids for every consensus, and validate bundle schema before finalization.

findings.finding: Broadcast entries exist but adoption is prompt-only: monitor counts ledgers, merger/agent paths do not enforce that bundles adopt broadcasts/consensus with required item_ids and source_refs.
evidence: stampede.sh prompt asks bundles to include adopted consensus item_ids/source_refs but no validator enforces it
findings.recommendation: Add adoption validation/consumption in commander bundle creation and merger synthesis; standardize adopted_consensus_item_ids.

recommended_fixes: Replace child-agents.jsonl write/truncate initialization with append-only idempotent registration.
Introduce a canonical atomic JSONL append helper with O_APPEND and one os.write per object line.
Add ledger schema validation for required fields, event-specific fields, timestamp format, and upstream item_id references.
Enhance monitor to parse JSONL and display schema-valid counts plus telemetry divergence warnings.
Define consensus promotion rules and enforce bundle adoption fields before merge/synthesis.
